description: This figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns of low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steels (x=0, 3, 6, denoted as 0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr respectively) fabricated via near-rapid solidification. Subplot (a) shows XRD profiles of as-cast steel strips, while subplot (b) shows profiles of strips after 600℃ annealing for 3 hours. The horizontal axis is diffraction angle 2θ ranging from 30° to 100°, and the vertical axis is diffraction intensity in arbitrary units. Detected phases include face-centered cubic γ austenite, body-centered cubic δ ferrite, and ordered DO3 phase, with corresponding characteristic peaks labeled. The intensity of δ ferrite/DO3 phase peaks rises with increasing Cr content, indicating higher volume fraction of δ ferrite in higher-Cr alloys. After annealing, more δ ferrite transforms into DO3 ordered phase, especially for 6Cr steel. This figure verifies that Cr addition promotes δ ferrite formation, and annealing facilitates δ ferrite to DO3 phase transition in this steel system.
